pages.raw_text:
Every man that can be employ
ed in Spokane is being rushed by
the Chicago, Milwaukee & st.
Paul as reinforcements for its con-
struction crews in Idaho and Wash
ington. Track laying and ballast
ing is proceeding on its main line
with speed unparalleled in the his
toryo railway building in this
country. If the present rate OF
progress is maintained the Mil
waukee will enter Seattle in time
to get a share of the exposition
business in the early part of next
summer.


Tracklaying has progress east
from Plummer, Idaho, to point
20 miles beyond st. Joe, distance
of 100 miles Plhmmer is seven
miles east of Tekoa. Wash, from
which point the Milwaukee will
work in and out of Spokane until
permanent arrangement are made
later for Spokane terminals. The
steel work near st. Joe is within
90 miles of the Montana line.


In Washington the steel crews
are working east from the Column
bia river for connection with the
Idaho work at Plummer. This
force is now at a point opposite
Sprague, Wash. Tracklaying ma
chimes are employed in the steel
work.


1t is expected that the three
miles tunnel being cut through the
great divide of the Rockies at Taft
Mont, on the ldaho-Montana line
will be completed in about siv
months. This tunnel is one of the
engineering marvels of the day.


Work is being rushed on the big
steel bridge across the Columbia
river at Priest rapids. On its com
pletion tracklaying will be pushed
west TO the Cascades. The work
in Montana is being conducted UNI
der a different management from
that in charge of the work in Ida
no and Washington, but is being
carried on with equal vigor.


The Milwaukee people are spar
ing neither men nor money in
building what they propose to make
the most substantial transcontinen
tai line in the country.
